observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the City Accountant strictly observe the use of appropriate inventory accounts for all the supplies and materials procured and subsequently recognize the related expense accounts when inventories are distributed or consumed in accordance with Paragraph 14 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards No. 12 and Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We have further reiterated the recommendation that the City Accountant require the end-users of the medicines and other medical, dental, and laboratories supplies to provide reports on utilization and other necessary information which will be the basis of the Accounting Office in determining the amount to be recognized as expense.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to reconcile records with the City Engineering Office (CEO) and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the completed projects to the appropriate PPE account. We have further recommended that the CEO furnish copies of the Certificates of Completion to the City Accounting Office for proper and timely recognition of completed projects.

We have recommended that the City Accountant exhaust all means to reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source agencies (SAs) and the LGU, prepare adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and require liquidation of the balances when necessary. If the sub-accounts were confirmed dormant, file a request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 which provide the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant fund transfers from National Government Agencies (NGAs)/SAs.

We have recommended that the City Accountant recognize all major repairs, improvements and procurement of major spare parts as part of PPE when these extend the service life, improve the capacity and efficiency or safety of the property and/or when major spare parts are directly installed and can be used only in connection with an item of PPE in accordance with Section 17 and 19 of IPSAS 17.

Further, we have recommended that the City Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to take up the understatement of the asset account and overstatement of the related expense account when necessary.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Treasurer’s Office to ensure consistency in the imposition of the LGU’s remedies on tax collections as required in Section 2A.08(B)(5a) of the Local Revenue and the existing collection efforts of the LGU in order to continually meet its target for the ensuing years and to consequently reduce delinquent accounts of taxpayers.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accounting Office, City Budget Office, City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office, City Health Office, focal persons and all departments and personnel concerned to: (1) faithfully adhere to the pertinent provisions of the underlying guidelines on the allocation, utilization and reporting of COVID-19 funds from all sources and the proper recognition in the appropriate accounts in the LGU books; (2) comply with the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of transferred funds; and (3) ensure the propriety of expenditures and that disbursements are adequately supported with necessary supporting documents.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Social Welfare Development Officer to encourage the People’s Organizations/Associations to settle the unliquidated funds totaling P232,000.00 so that funds will be utilized for other sustainable livelihood programs of the LGU. We have further recommended that the CSWDO ensure that specific provision on the liquidation of transferred funds be stipulated in the corresponding MOA to enforce payments from these POs/Associations as required in COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Engineering Office to require the contractor to expedite the completion of the school buildings in San Francisco Elementary School and Amoros Elementary School. We have further recommended that the Local School Board allocate sufficient funds from the SEF in the ensuing year for the full completion of the school buildings for the timely use of DepEd’s blended learning.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (CDRRMO) to regularly update the current year’s LDRRMF Investment Plan to include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF (QRF and DRRMF-MOOE) of the previous years.

We have further required the CDRRMO to submit a resolution from the Sangguniang Panlungsod on the declaration of the LGU under state of calamity of the Typhoon Odette to support the payments of financial assistance to affected households totaling P446,000.00 charged against the current year’s Quick Response Fund on or before February 28, 2022.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Health Office (CHO) through the City Accounting Office to submit the identified lacking documents particularly on the complete list of eligible health workers (HWs) and the total number of days physically present as certified by the Human Resource and Management Officer and the CHO and other sufficient evidence on the attendance of these HWs during the periods covered to support the validity of claims.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee and the City Engineering Office to submit the aforementioned pertinent contract documents for auditorial and technical review prior to the inspection of the completed project. We have further recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Head of the Procuring Entity, submit the required justification on the use of the Design and Build scheme on the construction of El Salvador City College.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Assessor’s Office to submit the identified lacking documents for further audit evaluation of the transactions. We have also recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that purchased lands of the City Government are covered with owner’s Transfer Certificates of Title and other satisfactory evidence to guarantee the integrity of land ownership and safeguard against third parties and future claimants.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee -Technical Working Group to take into consideration the thorough review of the technical requirements of the winning bidder during post-qualification stage particularly the competence and experience of the contractor’s personnel to be assigned in order to meet the expected date of completion of the project. We further recommend that the City Engineering Office require the contractor, MECC Builder, to expedite the completion of the projects otherwise, necessary liquidated damages will be imposed.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Housing Board (LHB) to enforce the terms and conditions of the Memorandum of Agreement by and between the LGU and the individual beneficiary of the relocation/resettlement sites and ensure that regular payments will be made by the beneficiaries to avoid the risk of default which will consequently lead to the termination of the contract agreement. We have further recommended that the LHB monitor payments made by beneficiaries and coordinate with the City Accounting Office for the appropriate posting in their respective accounts.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to strengthen the identification of programs and projects which are gender-responsive and specifically address a particular gender issue/s and be guided with Annex D of PCW–DILG–DBM–NEDA JMC No. 2013–01 on the expenses which are allowable to be charged against the GAD funds. We have further recommended the GFPS to consider the services of a gender experts/specialists for GAD-related trainings and activities in order to improve the formulation of the LGU’s annual GAD Plan and Budget.

We have also recommended that the LCE, through the City Budget Office and GFPS, to discontinue the existing practice of including programs on education and indigents in the GAD Plan and Budget. Ensure that the said programs form part of its regular budget in order to carry out the mandate of the LGU in providing basic services to its constituents.

We have recommended that the City Accountant set-up Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable in the books of accounts at the beginning of the year based on the RPT account register/taxpayer’s index card to be provided by the City Treasurer. 

Also, we required the City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant at the beginning of each year, a list of real property taxpayers with corresponding amount due and collectible for the current year as basis for the City Accounting Office to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable. 

We have also recommended that the City Government consider a policy formulation to establish a criteria for the provision of a reasonable allowance for impairment losses and the subsequent recording of the same in the books of accounts particularly the delinquent accounts.

Inquiry with the City Accountant also disclosed that the there are no provisions for a reasonable allowance since that City Treasurer expressed that their good office is determined to collect all receivables even the delinquent once.

compliance with Republic Act No. 9679.

We have recommended that the City Government require the recipient barangays to submit justification on utilizing the transferred funds for their meals and snacks.  Also, stipulate in the MOA a threshold percentage of the transferred funds to barangays which may be allowed for the meals and snacks and payments of allowances of barangay personnel serving as frontliners during the ongoing pandemic. Ensure that a maximum amount of the transferred funds shall be allocated for the procurement of essential items and other immediate needs to address the COVID-19 situation.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to submit justification on the inconsistency of contract duration stipulated in the contract agreement in working days against the one indicated during the procurement process, as clearly expressed in the Invitation to Bid, which is in calendar days. We further recommended that the BAC ensure that the instructions, forms, terms and specifications contained in the bidding documents are strictly adhered to. Otherwise, contracts executed which are inconsistent with the bidding documents are considered null and void.

We have recommended that the Office of the City Engineer submit the variation orders and other required supporting documents enumerated in COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 and Section 9.1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for further evaluation by the COA Regional Technical Services Unit prior to the conduct of final inspection of the completed project.

Also, consider the re-computation of the corresponding liquidated damages for the delays incurred by the contractor to be deducted from the retention money.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Engineering Office to submit justification on the significant delay incurred in the construction of the City College of El Salvador supported with an approved requests for time extension by the Head of Procuring Entity. We further recommend the City Engineering Office to oblige the contractor for the immediate completion of the project without prejudice to the imposition of liquidated damages for any delays incurred. Also, direct the concerned Project Management Team to take appropriate intervention and monitor closely the work activities to ensure immediate completion of the CCE.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Engineering Office to give priority in the implementation of the aforementioned projects as planned and to further require the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement process of the other remaining projects.

We have recommended that the City Government discontinue the grant of financial assistance to DepEd personnel. We further required the Local School Board through the Schools Division Superintendent (SDS) to submit the underlying DepEd guidelines on the grant of research development assistance amounting to P2,000.00 each to the 498 teaching and non-teaching personnel totaling P968,400.00 to substantiate the payment made.
We have also recommended the DepEd Division Office to attach the prevailing DepEd policies and guidelines to substantiate the disbursements for the different programs, projects and activities charged against the Special Education Fund.

We have recommended that the City DRRMO Head and the City Accountant require the concerned workers to submit monthly accomplishment reports and require the City DRRMO Head to certify whether the actual work accomplished were in accordance with the purpose of the program. We further required the respective Punong Barangay to submit justification on the utilization of the barangay response vehicles to cater other barangay transactions and operations.
We have also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office ensure that the LDRRMF shall be utilized in accordance with its intended purpose.

We have recommended that the City Accountant effect the mandatory deduction of a retention money or require the submission of a special bank guarantee by the supplier in favor of the City Government for every payment on the procurement of goods, to ensure that any manufacturing defects of the items procured be corrected by the supplier.
We have also recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to include the provision on the mandatory withholding of retention money ranging from 1% to 5% of the total contract price as part of the terms and condition in the bidding documents for compliance of the winning bidder in conformity with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 as amended by GPPB Resolution No. 30-2017.

Also, conduct regular physical count to ensure completeness of inventories on hand to confirm with the balances in the Stock Cards.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit a duly signed resolution requesting approval on the payment of CNA Incentive for FY 2020 by the City Rank and File Employees Association of El Salvador (CRAFEAES). We also required management to submit proof that the planned programs/activities/projects have been implemented of at least 70% accomplishment as of September 30, 2020. Also, we have further required the submission of the specific cost-cutting measures undertaken collectively by the management and the union which resulted to the savings generated from the allowable MOOE items where payment was source from.

We have recommended that the City Human Resource Management Office strictly impose the retention of the required 5-day vacation leave (VL) balance in the monetization of VL credits in accordance with Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No, 292 on the Omnibus Rules on Leave.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive discontinue the receipt of transportation allowance (TA) and cause immediate refund for the TA received in CY 2020 totaling P96,000.00.

We have recommended that the City Government discontinue the payment of honorarium to the city and barangay officials attending the monthly meetings of the Association of Barangay Council (ABC), Associations of Barangay Treasurer, Barangay Secretary and Barangay Record Keeper, Pag-asa Youth Association of the Philippines, Inc. (PYAP), City Development Council (CDC), Peace and Order Council (POC) and Anti-Drug Abuse Council (ADAC). We further required the City Government to submit a concrete legal basis on the payments of honoraria totaling P448,500.00 for meetings conducted in CY 2020.

We have recommended that the City General Services Office mark all government motor vehicles “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” three (3) inches in size and the name of the agency on both sides in compliance with Paragraph V(2) of COA Circular No. 75-6. We further required the GSO to provide a designated parking area/garage where all government vehicles will be kept when not in use after office hours and during holidays and weekends. Direct the Administrative Officer to assign a guard/LGU personnel who will monitor the in and out of all government vehicles to ensure that the use of the government vehicles are strictly for official business only.
We have also recommended the LGU to adapt effective measures to provide adequate control in the utilization and safekeeping of government motor vehicles.
